Add lightweight org-health metrics to the cockpit data layer. Work ONLY in sidecar/src/server.js and sidecar/test/console-parser.test.mjs. domain=backend. (1) Add an exported pure helper computeMetrics({ spendUsd, runs, ticketsTotal, ticketsDone }) that returns { spendUsd, runs, ticketsTotal, ticketsDone, costPerRun } where costPerRun = runs>0 ? Number((spendUsd/runs).toFixed(4)) : 0. (2) In snapshot(), compute spendUsd and runs (already available from the run table), and ticketsTotal = COUNT(*) of tickets, ticketsDone = COUNT(*) of tickets WHERE kanban_column='Done', then include metrics: computeMetrics(...) in the returned object. Do NOT change or remove any existing snapshot field, route, or behavior. (3) In sidecar/test/console-parser.test.mjs add assertions (keeping all existing ones) that computeMetrics({spendUsd:1,runs:4,ticketsTotal:2,ticketsDone:1}).costPerRun === 0.25 and .ticketsDone === 1. DONE WHEN: 'node sidecar/test/console-parser.test.mjs' prints PASS and 'node --check sidecar/src/server.js' exits 0.

Add a CYCLE PROGRESS stepper and surface the org-health metrics in the cockpit. Work ONLY in sidecar/web/ (index.html, style.css, app.js). (a) Add a compact horizontal stepper with a new container id 'cyclesteps' near the top of the dashboard, showing the council FSM phases in order: goal_intake, plan, ticket, assign, dev_done, test, audit, security, change_approval, goal_realign, stop. Highlight based on /api/state cycleState: phases before the current one = done, the current one = active, later = pending; use the steel-blue theme tokens (--primary #4682b4 for active/done, muted neutrals for pending). (b) Surface the new /api/state `metrics` object (costPerRun, runs, ticketsDone of ticketsTotal) as small tasteful stat chips near the existing spend readout in the header. Use the impeccable skill. domain=frontend. CRITICAL: preserve ALL existing element ids (agents, kanban, changes, feed, console, chat, goal, cycleState, spend, killBtn, conn) and the EventSource('/events') flow; only ADD (e.g. 'cyclesteps'), never remove/rename. DONE WHEN: 'node --check sidecar/web/app.js' exits 0 AND sidecar/web/index.html contains the ids agents, kanban, changes, feed, console, chat, cyclesteps.

Improve the cockpit ORG panel to show the COUNCIL reporting hierarchy instead of a flat list, so the Chair can see the chain of command. Work ONLY in sidecar/web/ (index.html, style.css, app.js). Using each agent's existing reports_to field from /api/state, render the #agents panel as an indented tree / grouped-by-manager view: the Chair at the top, agents grouped under whom they report to, preserving each agent's live status dot + current action. Visually FLAG the independent-oversight roles (auditor, security, compliance) — which report to 'chair' off the dev chain — as INDEPENDENT (a small tag or distinct steel-blue accent). Match the established steel-blue theme exactly; use the impeccable skill. domain=frontend. CRITICAL: keep the #agents id and ALL other existing ids (kanban, changes, feed, console, chat, goal, cycleState, spend, killBtn, conn, cyclesteps) and the EventSource flow intact; only refine the ORG panel's internal rendering. DONE WHEN: 'node --check sidecar/web/app.js' exits 0 AND sidecar/web/index.html still contains the ids agents, kanban, changes, feed, console, chat.
